payload.answer: Rejected. A large CSV/table should not be atomized into hundreds of durable memory cards by default. This is likely bulk data ingestion, not personal memory. Commit only explicit, durable, user-relevant facts with clear intent and provenance.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable personal-memory entity or specific user preference/fact is established by the proposal itself.

payload.intent: Validate a proposed bulk memory write of 500 cards derived one-per-row from a CSV/table.

details: Blocked bulk atomization of 500 CSV rows into memory cards.
No memory cards were approved for durable storage.

repair_options: Do not create 500 memory cards. Store the CSV externally or as an attached document/artifact if supported.
Ask the user which rows, if any, contain durable personal facts worth remembering.
Create a concise summary memory only if the user explicitly wants the table remembered and the summary is stable and useful.
Batch only a small number of high-salience facts after validation, preserving row identifiers/provenance.
